articles.title: IMD warns of heavy rain, thunderstorms across multiple states till July 2 - CNBC TV18
articles.full_text:
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has forecast an intensification of monsoon activity across large parts of the country over the next seven days, with widespread rainfall, thunderstorms, gusty winds and heavy to very heavy rain likely in several regions.
Heavy rainfall likely across multiple regions
Thunderstorms and strong winds expected
The weather agency has also warned of isolated thunderstorms accompanied by lightning and gusty winds of 40-60 kmph across several states, including Delhi, Punjab, Haryana, Uttar Pradesh, Rajasthan, Madhya Pradesh, Maharashtra, Odisha, Jharkhand, Bihar, Telangana, Andhra Pradesh, Kerala and Tamil Nadu. Thundersqualls with wind speeds of up to 70 kmph are likely over parts of Jharkhand and Bihar on June 26-27.
Very heavy rainfall alerts
Extremely heavy rainfall has been forecast over Sub-Himalayan West Bengal and Sikkim between June 27 and June 29. Very heavy rainfall is also likely over Arunachal Pradesh, Assam and Meghalaya, as well as Kerala and Mahe during the coming days.
Heatwave to continue in Uttar Pradesh
While monsoon activity strengthens elsewhere, heatwave conditions are likely to persist over isolated parts of Uttar Pradesh until June 28, with severe heatwave conditions expected on June 26 and 27. Hot and humid weather is also likely over Odisha on June 26 and 27.
Delhi-NCR forecast
For Delhi-NCR, the IMD has forecast partly cloudy skies with spells of light rain, thunderstorms, lightning and dust storms accompanied by strong winds of 40-50 kmph, gusting up to 60 kmph, on June 26 and 27. Maximum temperatures are expected to remain between 39°C and 42°C before gradually easing to 37°C-39°C by June 29.
Public and fishermen advised to stay alert
The IMD has advised residents in affected areas to remain indoors during thunderstorms, avoid taking shelter under trees, secure loose objects and stay away from water bodies and electrical installations during lightning activity.
The weather office has also warned fishermen against venturing into several parts of the Bay of Bengal and the Arabian Sea over the next few days due to rough sea conditions.
This version is more suited to a digital news article, with short, descriptive subheads that improve readability without changing the content.
Heavy rainfall likely across multiple regions
According to the IMD, fairly widespread to widespread rainfall is expected over parts of northwest, central, east, northeast, west and south peninsular India through July 2. Heavy rainfall is likely over Himachal Pradesh, Uttarakhand, Vidarbha, Chhattisgarh, Odisha, Bihar, Sub-Himalayan West Bengal and Sikkim, Arunachal Pradesh, Assam and Meghalaya, Konkan and Goa, Kerala, Coastal Karnataka, Telangana and parts of Tamil Nadu during the forecast period.
Thunderstorms and strong winds expected
The weather agency has also warned of isolated thunderstorms accompanied by lightning and gusty winds of 40-60 kmph across several states, including Delhi, Punjab, Haryana, Uttar Pradesh, Rajasthan, Madhya Pradesh, Maharashtra, Odisha, Jharkhand, Bihar, Telangana, Andhra Pradesh, Kerala and Tamil Nadu. Heatwave to continue in Uttar Pradesh
While monsoon activity strengthens elsewhere, heatwave conditions are likely to persist over isolated parts of Uttar Pradesh until June 28, with severe heatwave conditions expected on June 26 and 27. Hot and humid weather is also likely over Odisha on June 26 and 27.
Delhi-NCR forecast
For Delhi-NCR, the IMD has forecast partly cloudy skies with spells of light rain, thunderstorms, lightning and dust storms accompanied by strong winds of 40-50 kmph, gusting up to 60 kmph, on June 26 and 27. Maximum temperatures are expected to remain between 39°C and 42°C before gradually easing to 37°C-39°C by June 29.
